texto: In that light, philosophy is not so much--or not simply--'the love of wisdom,' but instead marks the passage from wonder as a noun to wonder as a verb. Philosophy is the love of wisdom to the extent that it remains an incitement to it.
explicacao: Citação de Michael Munro sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “In that light, philosophy is not so much--or not simply--'the love of wisdom,' but instead marks …” articula sabedoria, philosophy, wonder em registro de sabedoria, com relevância para contextos como sabedoria e wonder.

frase_original: In that light, philosophy is not so much--or not simply--'the love of wisdom,' but instead marks the passage from wonder as a noun to wonder as a verb. Philosophy is the love of wisdom to the extent that it remains an incitement to it.
